summary: Miscellaneous game-mode cleanup that improved names, constants, struct fields, and helper usage without changing generated code for GALE01. The PR replaced hard-coded controller counts with PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS, clarified time parameters as seconds, renamed gmMainLib_8046B0F0 fields for reset/progressive state, converted boolean integer writes to true/false, represented adjacent callback fields as an array, removed no-longer-needed dont_inline pragmas, and switched manual tick/second math to Dolphin OS time conversion helpers.
what_changed: Renamed gm_801692E8 and lbTime_8000B028 parameters from ticks to secs, reflecting that the values are seconds rather than raw OS ticks.
Changed lbTime_8000AFBC to use OSTicksToSeconds(OSGetTime()) instead of manual division by the bus clock-derived value at 0x800000F8.
Changed lbTime_8000B028 to use OSSecondsToTicks before OSTicksToCalendarTime instead of open-coded multiplication.
Replaced many hard-coded controller loop bounds of 4 with PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS across gm_16AE.c, gm_1A36.c, gm_1A45.c, and gm_1ADD.c.
Changed the controller aggregate slot in gm_1A36 from literal index 4 to PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS and declared controller_map.x0 as PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS + 1 in gm_1A36.static.h.
Converted repeated assignments over controller_map.x0[0..3] or [0..4] into loops over PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS or <= PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS.
Named gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x4 as resetting and gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x8 as progressive in src/melee/gm/types.h, then updated uses in gm_1A3F.c, gmmain_lib.c, and gmprogressive.c.
Changed gmMainLib_8046B0F0.xC from part of an int declaration to a bool field with a tentative comment: movie playback done, maybe?
Updated xC assignments and tests to use boolean style, such as gmMainLib_8046B0F0.xC = true/false and direct truth checks.
Simplified gmMainLib_8015FCC0 initialization by assigning x0 from OSGetResetCode() == 0x80000000 and setting resetting/progressive to false.
Refined gm_80479D58_t in gm_1A45.static.h by replacing separate callback members unk_14 and unk_18 with bool (*unk_14[2])(void), and updated gm_801A4B08/gm_801A4D34 call sites.
Changed gm_801A48A4 to iterate with ARRAY_SIZE(gm_803DA888) instead of literal 8, and removed a dont_inline pragma around it.
Removed a dont_inline pragma block around gm_801B9A3C in gmhomerun.c.
Changed a bit-clear operation from &= 0xFFFFFFFE to &= ~1 in gm_801A4970.
Renamed a temporary variable in gm_801A4D34 from temp_r3 to pad_queue_count, clarifying the lb_80019894 return value.
smart_moves: Using PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS centralized the controller-count assumption and made the special fifth aggregate controller slot explicit as PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS rather than magic index 4.
Declaring controller_map.x0 as PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS + 1 documents that the map stores four physical controllers plus one aggregate/all-controllers entry.
Renaming reset/progressive fields in gmMainLib_8046B0F0 tied anonymous offsets to observed behavior while preserving unknown names where evidence was weaker, such as x0 and xC.
Replacing manual timebase math with OSTicksToSeconds and OSSecondsToTicks made the code clearer and less error-prone while keeping the same behavior according to the decomp.dev report.
Converting repeated assignments into loops reduced duplication in controller setup while retaining matching output.
Representing two adjacent function pointers as an array allowed call sites to pass temp_r25->unk_14 directly instead of casting &temp_r25->unk_14 to void*.
Using ARRAY_SIZE(gm_803DA888) improved resilience to table-size changes and documented the loop as table-driven rather than a raw eight-iteration transform.
Removing dont_inline pragmas only after confirming the output stayed unchanged kept matching constraints from fossilizing into unnecessary source noise.
decomp_lessons: Prefer platform constants like PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS over literals when the literal represents a Dolphin/HSD API limit.
When a data structure has an extra aggregate/sentinel slot after per-controller entries, encode it as CONSTANT + 1 and use the constant as the aggregate index.
Offset-based struct members can often be safely renamed when all uses agree on behavior; here x4 became resetting and x8 became progressive.
Only promote an unknown field to a named boolean when uses support it; the PR left xC mostly anonymous but changed its type to bool and added a tentative comment.
Use bool values and direct boolean tests for bool fields instead of 0/1 comparisons when it does not disturb matching.
Adjacent callback fields may indicate an array; changing separate members to unk_14[2] can remove casts and better model call patterns.
Library or SDK helper macros/functions are preferable to open-coded hardware-register math when the helper expresses the same conversion.
Variable names like pad_queue_count can encode inferred semantics from producer functions and make frame-loop code easier to review.
Before preserving pragmas such as dont_inline, test whether the function still matches without them; stale pragmas can be removed as cleanup.
naming_conventions: Use semantic bool field names for gmMainLib_8046B0F0 members when behavior is known: resetting for reset-switch state and progressive for progressive/interlaced video mode.
Use secs rather than ticks when an integer time value represents seconds, including matching header prototypes.
Use PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS for physical controller loops and PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS + 1 for arrays that include an aggregate controller entry.
Prefer descriptive local names such as pad_queue_count over temp register-style names when the value's role is clear.
Use ARRAY_SIZE(table) for loops over static tables such as gm_803DA888.
Use boolean literals true and false for bool fields, and direct checks such as if (field) or if (!field).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The decomp.dev bot reported for GALE01: No changes, indicating the cleanup was matching-preserving.
Loops replaced repeated scalar assignments in gm_1A36 while still matching, showing that some manual unrolling was not required.
dont_inline pragmas were removed around gm_801A48A4 and gm_801B9A3C without reported codegen changes.
Manual OS timebase arithmetic was replaced with OSTicksToSeconds and OSSecondsToTicks without reported codegen changes.
Changing integer boolean assignments/comparisons to true/false and direct boolean conditions did not produce reported output changes.
Changing 0xFFFFFFFE to ~1 preserved the mask intent and apparently did not disturb matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only visible issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report: 'Report for GALE01 ... No changes.'
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining hard-coded controller loop bounds `i < 4` in gm or HSD pad code that could use PAD_MAX_CONTROLLERS.
Search for remaining literal controller aggregate index `4` in controller_map or gm_801A36A0 call sites.
Audit gmMainLib_8046B0F0.x0 and xC for stronger semantic names once more call sites are understood.
Search for open-coded OS timebase conversions using address 0x800000F8 that can be replaced with OSTicksToSeconds or OSSecondsToTicks.
Search for stale `#pragma dont_inline` blocks around already-matching functions that can be removed.
